channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: 5 shot near Howard University during homecoming week; 2 people in custody
item.description: Five people were hospitalized after a shooting near Howard University Friday, according to the DC Fire and EMS Department.
item.dc:creator: Kaitlyn Dillon, ABC 4

item.title: Photos of Melodee Buzzard 'look like her': Aunt
item.description: Melodee's aunt, Lizabeth Meza, joined "Banfield" on Friday night and said she had not seen Melodee for over four years.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Trump heads to 3-country sprint in Asia, meeting with Xi as government shutdown drags on at home
item.description: ABOARD AIR FORCE ONE (AP) — President Donald Trump headed for Asia Friday night for the first time this term, a trip where he’s expected to work on investment deals and peace efforts before meeting face-to-face with Chinese President Xi Jinping to try to deescalate a trade war.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE and MARK SCHIEFELBEIN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Anti-abortion pregnancy centers are looking to offer much more than ultrasounds and diapers
item.description: More anti-abortion pregnancy centers have been offering medical services such as testing for sexually transmitted infections.
item.dc:creator: Geoff Mulvihill And Kimberlee Kruesi, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump ends trade talks with Canada over tariffs ad that Ontario premier now says he'll phase out
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump announced he’s ending “all trade negotiations” with Canada because of a television ad sponsored by one of its provinces that used the words of former President Ronald Reagan to criticize U.S. tariffs — prompting the province's leader to later pull the ad.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, SEUNG MIN KIM and ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Tropical Storm Melissa stationary in the Caribbean as 4 deaths reported and huge rains expected
item.description: SAN JUAN, Puerto Rico (AP) — Tropical Storm Melissa was nearly stationary in the central Caribbean on Friday as forecasters warned it could strengthen and swipe Jamaica as a powerful hurricane and dump a staggering amount of rain — up to 35 inches (89 centimeters) — on southwest Haiti, where they wa
item.dc:creator: DÁNICA COTO, JOHN MYERS JR. and EVENS SANON, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump’s Asia return and East Timor’s entry take spotlight in landmark ASEAN summit
item.description: KUALA LUMPUR, Malaysia (AP) — Southeast Asian foreign ministers opened talks Saturday ahead of a landmark ASEAN summit that will formally welcome East Timor as the bloc’s 11th member and mark U.S. President Donald Trump’s first trip to Asia since returning to the White House.
item.dc:creator: EILEEN NG, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump speaks at tele-rally to boost GOP's chances in governor's race in New Jersey
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump urged his supporters in New Jersey late Friday to cast a ballot for the Republican candidate for governor, Jack Ciattarelli, before early voting starts in the state on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Japan's new leader faces diplomatic gauntlet with Trump, China and regional summits
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Just days after taking office, Japan's new leader faces a series of back-to-back foreign policy tests, with a meeting with U.S. President Donald Trump in Tokyo sandwiched between Asia-region summits in Malaysia and South Korea.
item.dc:creator: KEN MORITSUGU and MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Ontario premier says he'll pull ad that upset Trump so trade talks between Canada and US can resume
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — The leader of Canada’s most populous province said Friday he’ll pull the anti-tariff ad that prompted U.S. President Donald Trump to end trade talks with Canada.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Shots fired, driver hurt and held after truck rams into Coast Guard base in California
item.description: A truck driver who backed into a U.S. Coast Guard base in the San Francisco Bay Area — the site of earlier protests against federal immigration agents — was shot by law enforcement officers and wounded, authorities said Friday.
item.dc:creator: KATHY McCORMACK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: PHOTOS: Thieves cut through roof, steal hundreds of shoes in Florida mall heist
item.description: Florida authorities are searching for suspects who investigators said pulled off an elaborate heist to steal thousands of dollars in shoes from a Martin County mall this week.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Unger, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Walmart is changing how hourly store workers earn raises
item.description: The retail giant's new approach will take employee performance into account as well as years of service.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, KREX 5

item.title: Girl shot in Minneapolis church returns home from the hospital
item.description: A 12-year-old girl who was shot in the head during a deadly attack at a Minneapolis Catholic church in August was released from the hospital Thursday and greeted with cheers as the police chief paraded her around town in a stretch limousine, according to the Minneapolis Star Tribune.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump says tech leaders and mayor changed his mind about using federal force in San Francisco
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — President Donald Trump was geared up for a show of federal force in San Francisco, a city he’s blasted as everything wrong with liberal governance. Then conversations with some of the Bay Area’s most prominent tech leaders and the mayor changed his mind.
item.dc:creator: JANIE HAR, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Officials investigating after deadly crane collapse in commercial area north of Boston
item.description: Authorities, including officials from OSHA, are investigating after a fatal crane collapse at a commercial site north of Boston in Everett, Massachusetts.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Bill to fund SNAP in shutdown introduced in House
item.description: Rep. Mariannette Miller-Meeks (R-Iowa) on Friday introduced a bill to fund the Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP) as the government shutdown threatens payments of the food aid in some states.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, KRON4

item.title: Rosa Parks and Helen Keller statues unveiled at the Alabama Capitol
item.description: MONTGOMERY, Ala. (AP) — Statues of Rosa Parks and Helen Keller, pivotal figures who fought for justice and inspired change across the world, were unveiled Friday on the grounds of the Alabama Capitol.
item.dc:creator: KIM CHANDLER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 7 charged in 2024 Pennsylvania voter registration fraud that prosecutors say was motivated by money
item.description: HARRISBURG, Pa. (AP) — A yearlong investigation into suspected fraudulent voter registration forms submitted ahead of last year's presidential election produced criminal charges Friday against six street canvassers and the man who led their work in Pennsylvania.
item.dc:creator: MARK SCOLFORO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: DOJ prepares to send election monitors to California, New Jersey following requests from state GOPs
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — The Department of Justice is preparing to send federal election observers to California and New Jersey next month, targeting two Democratic states holding off-year elections following requests from state Republican parties.
item.dc:creator: JILL COLVIN and MICHAEL R. BLOOD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Reagan ad that infuriated Trump set to run during World Series
item.description: Ontario Premier Doug Ford said on Friday that the anti-tariffs advertisement featuring former President Reagan, which angered President Trump, will air during the World Series before being paused on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Mancini, WGN 9

item.title: US says it now plans to deport Abrego Garcia to Liberia as soon as Oct. 31
item.description: BALTIMORE (AP) — The U.S. government plans to deport Kilmar Abrego Garcia to Liberia, and could do so as early as Oct. 31, according to a Friday court filing.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Richard Mille watches worth 'hundreds of thousands of dollars' stolen during F1 weekend
item.description: “The group targeted wealthy individuals that were wearing high-end luxury watches,” court documents said.
item.dc:creator: Julianna Russ, NewsNation

item.title: In emotional speech, Zohran Mamdani defends Muslim identity against 'racist and baseless' attacks
item.description: Zohran Mamdani, the Democratic nominee for New York City mayor, is pledging to embrace his Muslim identity amid growing attacks in the final days of the campaign.
item.dc:creator: Jake Offenhartz, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Woman arrested after admitting to fabricating burglary, assault story
item.description: Upon further investigation, detectives found inconsistencies between her statements and newly obtained evidence.
item.dc:creator: Luisa Barrios, NewsNation

item.title: New York man accused of killing estranged wife with cyanide-like powder
item.description: Aleena Asif's 18-year-old daughter called 911 because she was concerned when her mom didn't pick up her 7-year-old sister from school.
item.dc:creator: Mira Wassef, NewsNation

item.title: Judge halts death penalty case in New Mexico due to federal shutdown
item.description: SANTA FE, N.M. (AP) — A judge in New Mexico has halted a federal death penalty case, citing the pinch of the U.S. government shutdown and a funding shortfall for specialized public defenders that stretches back to the summer.
item.dc:creator: MORGAN LEE and JACQUES BILLEAUD, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Cracker Barrel CEO explains reason for attempt to change logo: report
item.description: Cracker Barrel had debuted its new logo in August, but quickly faced backlash for ditching a depiction of the “Old Timer” previously seen on the sign.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Where the jewels stolen from the Louvre Museum might end up
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Just days after a stunning heist at the Louvre Museum in Paris, speculation is growing around where the lavish, stolen jewels that once adorned France's royals might end up.
item.dc:creator: WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS and R.J. RICO, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: A timeline of US attacks off South America and what Congress has had to say
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — In less than two months, President Donald Trump and Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth say the U.S. military has killed at least 37 people in nine strikes against drug-smuggling vessels in the Caribbean Sea and eastern Pacific Ocean.
item.dc:creator: BEN FINLEY and KONSTANTIN TOROPIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 5 times the White House underwent major renovations
item.description: President Trump's construction of a grand ballroom attached to the White House — starting with the demolition of part of the East Wing this week — will not be the first time a president has left a lasting mark on the people's house.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KRON4

item.title: What is 'ghost tapping?': New scheme targeting tap-to-pay users
item.description: The Better Business Bureau is warning tap-to-pay users of a new scheme that could make simple transactions costly.
item.dc:creator: Katlyn Fernandez, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Democrats look to long term as North Carolina GOP redistricting plan seeks another seat for Trump
item.description: RALEIGH, N.C. (AP) — Democrats rallied Tuesday against a new U.S. House map proposed by North Carolina Republicans that would likely reap another GOP seat at President Donald Trump's behest, but the critics acknowledged they'll likely be unable to halt the redraw for now.
item.dc:creator: MAKIYA SEMINERA and GARY D. ROBERTSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Texas couple accused of moving, leaving child behind
item.description: A 12-year-old Texas boy came home from school to find it empty and his family gone, according to police.
item.dc:creator: Roland Richter, Fox 5

item.title: Molson Coors to eliminate 9% of its workforce in the Americas amid slowing sales
item.description: Molson Coors announced its plans to restructure — and eliminate 400 of its salaried positions as part of the process.
item.dc:creator: Heather Willard, WGN 9

item.title: France's former President Sarkozy will begin serving a 5-year prison sentence Tuesday
item.description: Nicolas Sarkozy will become the first former French president in living memory to be imprisoned when he is expected to begin a five-year sentence Tuesday in Paris’ La Santé prison.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY SCHAEFFER and NICOLAS GARRIGA, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Philadelphia police find human remains in search for missing woman
item.description: PHILADELPHIA (AP) — Philadelphia police found human remains Saturday in their search for a 23-year-old woman who disappeared from her nursing home job about two weeks ago.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4
